channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-06-12T20:03:54.045Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cross-border spread to Uganda. Per WHO Disease Outbreak News DON606 (data as of 6 June 2026), the DRC has reported 515 confirmed cases and 91 confirmed deaths (CFR 17.7%), and Uganda has reported 19 confirmed cases (2 deaths) plus one probable case who died — 534 confirmed cases and 93 deaths in total (overall CFR 17.4%), with at least 17 people recovered. WHO notes the reported CFR is likely an underestimate, as many deaths occurring before the outbreak declaration remain under investigation. Confirmed cases have risen sharply from the late-May DON605 snapshot (125 confirmed) as laboratory testing reclassified earlier suspected cases. The regional response has escalated: Uganda border health measures, a contested US-supported quarantine/care arrangement in Kenya, a Brazilian investigation of possible imported cases, and accelerated multi-candidate vaccine efforts. WHO updates these figures from DRC government (COUSP-DRC) situation reports and they are subject to retrospective revision — verify the latest WHO situation report before operational use.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (12 confirmed, 1 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 2,073 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of June 11), representing 30 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Yellow fever — Colombia (Tolima) & the Americas
item.description: PAHO reports sustained yellow fever transmission across parts of South America, with cases expanding beyond traditional Amazonian risk zones. The department of Tolima, Colombia — where human cases had not been seen in over a century — is the current epicenter and concentrates the highest case and death counts in the country. Transmission is driven overwhelmingly by unvaccinated individuals. Verify current national figures against PAHO and Colombia's Instituto Nacional de Salud (INS) before publication; counts are revised as cases are laboratory-confirmed.

item.description: Breakthrough in Single-dose Vaccine for Andes Hantavirus Labcompare

item.title: NPR: What would it take to stop women from bleeding to death after childbirth?
item.description: A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.

item.title: Science News: A popular sunscreen ingredient can finally be sold in the United States
item.description: The FDA will allow bemotrizinol in sunscreen. The chemical is long-lasting and defends against solar radiation that ages skin.

item.description: Dutch expedition ship Hondius resumes cruises after fatal hantavirus outbreak NL Times

item.title: CBC News: B.C. birth alerts class-action lawsuit leads to $66M proposed settlement
item.description: A class-action lawsuit filed on behalf of people who were the subject of birth alerts, which shared private information without the person's knowledge, has reached a $66-million proposed settlement, according to class counsel.

item.title: Google News: Forget hantavirus — another rat disease killed a California resident after 200 rodents found in home - New York Post
item.description: Forget hantavirus — another rat disease killed a California resident after 200 rodents found in home New York Post

item.description: Bay Area still battling 'extremely contagious' norovirus SFGATE
